_metadata.title: CMS Place of Service (POS) Codes
_metadata.source: CMS Place of Service Code Set (https://www.cms.gov/Medicare/Coding/place-of-service-codes)
_metadata.description: Two-digit numeric codes used on the CMS-1500 (item 24B) and 837P EDI to indicate where a service was rendered.

01.name: Pharmacy
01.description: A facility or location where drugs and other medically related items and services are sold, dispensed, or otherwise provided directly to patients.

02.name: Telehealth Provided Other than in Patient's Home
02.description: The location where health services and health related services are provided or received, through telecommunication technology. Patient is not located in their home when receiving health services or health related services through telecommunication technology.

03.name: School
03.description: A facility whose primary purpose is education.

04.name: Homeless Shelter
04.description: A facility or location whose primary purpose is to provide temporary housing to homeless individuals.

05.name: Indian Health Service Free-standing Facility
05.description: A facility or location, owned and operated by the Indian Health Service, providing diagnostic, therapeutic, and rehabilitation services to American Indians and Alaska Natives who do not require hospitalization.

06.name: Indian Health Service Provider-based Facility
06.description: A facility or location, owned and operated by the Indian Health Service, providing diagnostic, therapeutic, and rehabilitation services rendered by, or under the supervision of, physicians to American Indians and Alaska Natives admitted as inpatients or outpatients.

07.name: Tribal 638 Free-standing Facility
07.description: A facility or location owned and operated by a federally recognized American Indian or Alaska Native tribe or tribal organization under a 638 agreement, providing diagnostic, therapeutic, and rehabilitation services to tribal members who do not require hospitalization.

08.name: Tribal 638 Provider-based Facility
08.description: A facility or location owned and operated by a federally recognized American Indian or Alaska Native tribe or tribal organization under a 638 agreement, providing diagnostic, therapeutic, and rehabilitation services to tribal members admitted as inpatients or outpatients.

09.name: Prison/Correctional Facility
09.description: A prison, jail, reformatory, work farm, detention center, or any other similar facility maintained by either Federal, State or local authorities for the purpose of confinement or rehabilitation.

10.name: Telehealth Provided in Patient's Home
10.description: The location where health services and health related services are provided or received, through telecommunication technology. Patient is located in their home when receiving health services or health related services through telecommunication technology.

11.name: Office
11.description: Location, other than a hospital, skilled nursing facility, military treatment facility, community health center, State or local public health clinic, or intermediate care facility, where the health professional routinely provides health examinations, diagnosis, and treatment of illness or injury on an ambulatory basis.

12.name: Home
12.description: Location, other than a hospital or other facility, where the patient receives care in a private residence.

13.name: Assisted Living Facility
13.description: Congregate residential facility with self-contained living units providing assessment of each resident's needs and on-site support 24 hours a day.

14.name: Group Home
14.description: A residence, with shared living areas, where clients receive supervision and other services such as social and/or behavioral services, custodial service, and minimal services (e.g. medication administration).

15.name: Mobile Unit
15.description: A facility/unit that moves from place-to-place equipped to provide preventive, screening, diagnostic, and/or treatment services.

16.name: Temporary Lodging
16.description: A short-term accommodation such as a hotel, camp ground, hostel, cruise ship or resort where the patient receives care, and which is not identified by any other POS code.

17.name: Walk-in Retail Health Clinic
17.description: A walk-in health clinic, other than an office, urgent care facility, pharmacy or independent clinic and not described by any other Place of Service code, that is located within a retail operation and provides, on an ambulatory basis, preventive and primary care services.

18.name: Place of Employment-Worksite
18.description: A location, not described by any other POS code, owned or operated by a public or private entity where the patient is employed, and where a health professional provides on-going or episodic occupational medical, therapeutic or rehabilitative services to the individual.

19.name: Off Campus-Outpatient Hospital
19.description: A portion of an off-campus hospital provider based department which provides diagnostic, therapeutic (both surgical and nonsurgical), and rehabilitation services to sick or injured persons who do not require hospitalization or institutionalization.

20.name: Urgent Care Facility
20.description: Location, distinct from a hospital emergency room, an office, or a clinic, whose purpose is to diagnose and treat illness or injury for unscheduled, ambulatory patients seeking immediate medical attention.

21.name: Inpatient Hospital
21.description: A facility, other than psychiatric, which primarily provides diagnostic, therapeutic (both surgical and nonsurgical), and rehabilitation services by, or under, the supervision of physicians to patients admitted for a variety of medical conditions.

22.name: On Campus-Outpatient Hospital
22.description: A portion of a hospital's main campus which provides diagnostic, therapeutic (both surgical and nonsurgical), and rehabilitation services to sick or injured persons who do not require hospitalization or institutionalization.

23.name: Emergency Room - Hospital
23.description: A portion of a hospital where emergency diagnosis and treatment of illness or injury is provided.

24.name: Ambulatory Surgical Center
24.description: A freestanding facility, other than a physician's office, where surgical and diagnostic services are provided on an ambulatory basis.

25.name: Birthing Center
25.description: A facility, other than a hospital's maternity facilities or a physician's office, which provides a setting for labor, delivery, and immediate post-partum care as well as immediate care of new born infants.

26.name: Military Treatment Facility
26.description: A medical facility operated by one or more of the Uniformed Services. Military Treatment Facility (MTF) also refers to certain former U.S. Public Health Service (USPHS) facilities now designated as Uniformed Service Treatment Facilities (USTF).

27.name: Outreach Site/Street
27.description: A non-permanent location on the street or found environment, not described by any other POS code, where health professionals provide preventive, screening, diagnostic, and/or treatment services to unsheltered homeless individuals.

31.name: Skilled Nursing Facility
31.description: A facility which primarily provides inpatient skilled nursing care and related services to patients who require medical, nursing, or rehabilitative services but does not provide the level of care or treatment available in a hospital.

32.name: Nursing Facility
32.description: A facility which primarily provides to residents skilled nursing care and related services for the rehabilitation of injured, disabled, or sick persons, or, on a regular basis, health-related care services above the level of custodial care to other than individuals with intellectual disabilities.

33.name: Custodial Care Facility
33.description: A facility which provides room, board and other personal assistance services, generally on a long-term basis, and which does not include a medical component.

34.name: Hospice
34.description: A facility, other than a patient's home, in which palliative and supportive care for terminally ill patients and their families are provided.

41.name: Ambulance - Land
41.description: A land vehicle specifically designed, equipped and staffed for lifesaving and transporting the sick or injured.

42.name: Ambulance - Air or Water
42.description: An air or water vehicle specifically designed, equipped and staffed for lifesaving and transporting the sick or injured.

49.name: Independent Clinic
49.description: A location, not part of a hospital and not described by any other Place of Service code, that is organized and operated to provide preventive, diagnostic, therapeutic, rehabilitative, or palliative services to outpatients only.

50.name: Federally Qualified Health Center
50.description: A facility located in a medically underserved area that provides Medicare beneficiaries preventive primary medical care under the general direction of a physician.

51.name: Inpatient Psychiatric Facility
51.description: A facility that provides inpatient psychiatric services for the diagnosis and treatment of mental illness on a 24-hour basis, by or under the supervision of a physician.

52.name: Psychiatric Facility-Partial Hospitalization
52.description: A facility for the diagnosis and treatment of mental illness that provides a planned therapeutic program for patients who do not require full time hospitalization, but who need broader programs than are possible from outpatient visits to a hospital-based or hospital-affiliated facility.

53.name: Community Mental Health Center
53.description: A facility that provides comprehensive mental health services on an ambulatory basis primarily to individuals residing or employed in a defined area.

54.name: Intermediate Care Facility/Individuals with Intellectual Disabilities
54.description: A facility which primarily provides health-related care and services above the level of custodial care to individuals with intellectual disabilities but does not provide the level of care or treatment available in a hospital or SNF.

55.name: Residential Substance Abuse Treatment Facility
55.description: A facility which provides treatment for substance (alcohol and drug) abuse to live-in residents who do not require acute medical care.

56.name: Psychiatric Residential Treatment Center
56.description: A facility or distinct part of a facility for psychiatric care which provides a total 24-hour therapeutically planned and professionally staffed group living and learning environment.

57.name: Non-residential Substance Abuse Treatment Facility
57.description: A location which provides treatment for substance (alcohol and drug) abuse on an ambulatory basis.

58.name: Non-residential Opioid Treatment Facility
58.description: A location that provides treatment for opioid use disorder on an ambulatory basis.

60.name: Mass Immunization Center
60.description: A location where providers administer pneumococcal pneumonia and influenza virus vaccinations and submit these services as electronic media claims, paper claims, or using the roster billing method.

61.name: Comprehensive Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility
61.description: A facility that provides comprehensive rehabilitation services under the supervision of a physician to inpatients with physical disabilities.

62.name: Comprehensive Outpatient Rehabilitation Facility
62.description: A facility that provides comprehensive rehabilitation services under the supervision of a physician to outpatients with physical disabilities.

65.name: End-Stage Renal Disease Treatment Facility
65.description: A facility other than a hospital, which provides dialysis treatment, maintenance, and/or training to patients or caregivers on an ambulatory or home-care basis.

71.name: Public Health Clinic
71.description: A facility maintained by either State or local health departments that provides ambulatory primary medical care under the general direction of a physician.

72.name: Rural Health Clinic
72.description: A certified facility which is located in a rural medically underserved area that provides ambulatory primary medical care under the general direction of a physician.

81.name: Independent Laboratory
81.description: A laboratory certified to perform diagnostic and/or clinical tests independent of an institution or a physician's office.

99.name: Other Place of Service
99.description: Other place of service not identified above.
